generated_comment: Arey bhai, har ghar mein jo jisme better ho wahi karta hai. Mere husband bahar ke kaam, bank-vank aur bill sab dekh lete hain, aur ghar, rasoi, bachchon ki cheezein aurat hi achchhe se sambhal leti hai. Aajkal ke ladke thoda chai bana dein ya bachche ko ready kar dein toh log bolte hain wah kya modern pati hai, par asli zimmedari toh ghar ki aurat par hi suit karti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as practical and inherently suited to men and women. It encodes efficiency framing, 'he handles outside/finances, she handles home,' and praises minimal male domestic contribution as exceptional.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. You are at a kitty party with seven women from your neighbourhood. One woman mentions that her niece who is 29 just got engaged. Everyone congratulates her and someone says thank god, it was getting late. The conversation turns to late marriages in general and what happens to women who wait too long and what the right age is and what parents are doing wrong when their daughters reach 28 and 29 still unmarried. You are participating in this conversation and giving your opinion on what the correct window for a woman's marriage is and what closes after that window shuts.
